metadata.notes: EBU Level 4 system. Brown/Red sticker. Precision-style 1!c (may be short), strong 1!d, Level 4 majors, multi-meaning 2-level openings.

general-swiss.name: General Swiss
general-swiss.description: 4!c/4!d control-point slam try once GF established and suit agreed. A=2, K/singleton/Q-of-trumps=1. 11 CP = small slam, 13 = grand. Adjust: +2 if GF shown by both, -2 if one hand was weak (weak opening or 0-7 response to 1!d).

meaning.description: Sign off — at most 4 CPs combined, no slam interest
Exactly 8 CPs — sign off in small slam
Sign off — at most 3 CPs combined, no slam interest
Exactly 7 CPs — sign off small slam

rkcb.name: Roman Key Card Blackwood 1430
rkcb.description: 4NT asks for key cards (4 aces + K of agreed suit). 1430 responses.

meaning.description: Roman Key Card Blackwood — asks for key cards (4 aces + K!{suit})
Queen ask — do you have Q!{suit}?
Q!{suit} + K!s (only if !s not agreed suit)
Queen ask — do you have Q!{suit}?

rkqg.name: Roman Key-Quant Gerber
rkqg.description: 4!c over NT asks for aces with quantitative follow-up option.

meaning.description: Roman Key-Quant Gerber — asks aces, allows quantitative slam invite
Accept quantitative — small slam in NT
Accept quantitative — small slam in NT

viscount.name: Viscount
viscount.description: 4NT over NT = bid 4-card suits up the line (Baron-style). Alert.

meaning.description: Viscount — bid 4-card suits up the line (like Baron); slam investigation in NT
4+ !s, no 4-card suit below (flat hand, 4333 or similar)

keri.name: Keri (5-card puppet)
keri.description: 2!c over 1NT = 5-card puppet Keri. Announced 'Keri'. Covers weak hands wanting clubs/diamonds, and any hand wanting to find a 5-3 major fit.

meaning.description: Keri — puppet to 2!d; various hand types
Weak hand with diamonds — to play 2!d
4+ !s; may also have 4 !h. Opener now bids naturally.
Opener has 5-card !h — shows instead of relaying
Forcing inquiry — fit or other hand
Opener has 5-card !s — shows instead of relaying

1nt-resp.name: 1NT Continuations
1nt-resp.description: Continuations after any natural 1NT opening. 2!c=Keri (separate ref). Transfers to !h/!s/!d, 2!s range/!c, minor slam tries, 5-5 majors.

meaning.description: 4-card !h support, maximum, no side 4-card suit — super-accept
4-card !s support, maximum, no side 4-card suit — super-accept
Range enquiry OR inv+ transfer to !c (ambiguous until rebid)
Minimum 1NT — opener sign-off or invite declined
Any maximum (pass if 2!s showed clubs; continue otherwise)
Long suit needing help to run (slam try or 3NT probe) — opener passes with Kxx+ or bids 3NT
To play 3!c — no fit, no cover card
Cover card in !c — good to play 3NT
Slam try in !c — asks opener for help
Small doubleton in !c — not suitable for !c slam
Slam try in !d — asks opener for help
Small doubleton in !d — not suitable for !d slam

2nt-resp.name: 2NT Continuations
2nt-resp.description: Continuations after any natural 2NT (20-23 balanced, or as opener's rebid). 5-card puppet Stayman, transfers, minor preference, RKQG, Viscount.

meaning.description: 5-card puppet Stayman — asks for 5-card major or 4-card major
To play 3NT (no !h fit, or prefers NT)
Puppet to 3NT — either to play or slam try in a minor
5!s + 4!h — to play in 3NT or explore game

1c-resp.name: 1!c Responses (1st/2nd seat)
1c-resp.description: Responses to 1!c (12-15 balanced or 10-15 unbalanced no 5-card major ≤1 four-card major). 1!d=0-8 relay; 1!h=9-15 any; 1!s=15+ GF; 1NT/2x=transfer weak jump shifts.

meaning.description: 12-15 unbalanced, exactly 4!h (4441 or longer minor canape)
12-15 unbalanced, exactly 4!s (4441 or longer minor canape)
Balanced, no game interest — natural non-forcing weak takeout

1d-resp.name: 1!d Responses (suction relays)
1d-resp.description: Responses to 1!d (16+, alert, not necessarily GF). 1!h=0-7 relay; 1!s=8+ GF with suction relay rebids; 1NT/2x=transfer weak jump shifts.

meaning.description: Natural — 5+!s (may have longer minor, canape possible)
8+ game forcing relay — asks opener to describe via suction rebids
Suction: !d single-suiter OR !h+!s two-suiter OR !d+!h+!s three-suiter (16-21)
Single-suited !d — relay further to ask HCP range
Agree !h — slam try; Swiss/RKCB to follow
Agree !s — slam try; Swiss/RKCB to follow
Suction: !h single-suiter OR !s+!c two-suiter OR !h+!s+!c three-suiter (16-21)
Suction: !s single-suiter OR !c+!d two-suiter OR !s+!c+!d three-suiter (16-21)
Suction: !c single-suiter OR !h+!d two-suiter OR !c+!h+!d three-suiter (16-21)
Relay — asks opener to clarify (3!c = further enquiry)

1h-l4-resp.name: 1!h (Level 4) Responses
1h-l4-resp.description: Responses to 1!h L4 — 10-15 unbalanced, 4+ !h, may have longer minor (canape). Alert bid.

meaning.description: Good raise to 3!h or slam try in !h — forces to game or invites slam
Extras but no suitable trial or cue — to play
Fit jump — good raise + good side !s suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !c suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !d suit
Good raise to 4!h — 4-card support, balanced values

1s-l4-resp.name: 1!s (Level 4) Responses
1s-l4-resp.description: Responses to 1!s L4 — 10-15 unbalanced, 5+ !s, may have longer minor (canape). Alert bid.

meaning.description: Good raise to 3!s or slam try in !s — forces to game or invites slam
Extras but no suitable trial or cue — to play
Fit jump — good raise + good side !c suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !d suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !h suit
Good raise to 4!s — 4-card support, balanced values

2c-resp.name: 2!c Responses
2c-resp.description: Responses to 2!c multi — weak !d (6+, 5-9) OR weak !h+!s 5-4+ (5-9) OR 20-23 balanced OR GF !h+!s 5-4+ (3 losers).

meaning.description: Pass or correct — prefers !d; opener passes with weak !d, rebids otherwise
Non-forcing — major focused; pass if min weak majors (!h preference)
Non-forcing — major focused; pass if min weak majors (!s preference)
Forcing 1 round — how good are your diamonds?

2d-resp.name: 2!d Responses
2d-resp.description: Responses to 2!d multi — weak !h (6+, 5-9) OR weak !s+!c 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !s+!c 5-4+ (3 losers).

2h-resp.name: 2!h Responses
2h-resp.description: Responses to 2!h multi — weak !s (6+, 5-9) OR weak !c+!d 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !c+!d 5-4+ (3 losers).

2s-resp.name: 2!s Responses
2s-resp.description: Responses to 2!s multi — weak !c (7+, 5-9) OR weak !h+minor 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !h+minor 5-4+ (3 losers).

2nt-multi-resp.name: 2NT (multi) Responses
2nt-multi-resp.description: Responses to 2NT opening — weak !s+!d 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !s+!d 5-4+ (3 losers) OR 24+ balanced.

meaning.description: Simple preference — pass or rebid for weak two-suiter
Preemptive raise !d (any rebid by opener = GF)
Preemptive raise !s (any rebid by opener = GF)
Transfer preference invitational for !h (opener breaks if GF two-suiter)
Transfer preference invitational for !s (opener breaks if GF two-suiter)
Transfer preference mixed raise — !h side interest
Transfer preference mixed raise — !s side interest

3xfer-resp.name: 3!c/3!d/3!h Transfer Responses
3xfer-resp.description: Responses to 3!c (GF !c or preempt !d), 3!d (GF !d or preempt !h), 3!h (GF !h or preempt !s). Complete transfer = to play vs weak; step above = shortage ask; 3NT vs weak = to play; 4!c/4!d = Swiss for weak option.

meaning.description: Complete transfer — to play vs weak option; no extras
Shortage ask (one step above completing transfer) — are you weak or GF? If weak: 3NT=no shortage; 4x=shortage in that suit; If GF: GF responses
To play 3NT (vs weak option only; GF explores further)
General Swiss for weak option in transfer suit
General Swiss for weak option in transfer suit
Complete transfer to 4!h — to play (after 4!c opening)
Complete transfer to 4!s — to play (after 4!d opening)
Slam try with support (after 4!c): opener completes = min; cue bids = max with cue
Slam try with support (after 4!d): opener completes = min; cue bids = max with cue

4nt-aces-resp.name: 4NT Specific Aces Responses
4nt-aces-resp.description: Responses to 4NT opening — specific ace ask. Opener missing ≤2 aces or void.

lebensohl.name: Lebensohl (over 1NT overcalled)
lebensohl.description: After (1NT)-(2x) or our 1NT overcalled: 2-level bids NF; 3-level GF; direct cue=Staymanic no stop; 3NT no stop; 2NT puppet to 3!c for slower sequences.

meaning.description: Puppet to 3!c — allows invite or stop-showing sequences
To play 3NT — promises stop in overcalled suit
Direct cue bid — Staymanic, denies stop
To play 3NT — denies stop in overcalled suit

michaels-resp.name: Michaels Cue Responses
michaels-resp.description: After our Michaels cue bid (5-5+ in two suits, weak or strong). Over minor = both majors; over major = other major + either minor.

meaning.description: Pass or correct — simple preference for known suit at lowest level
Invitational+ enquiry for other suit / strength
Re-cue = strong hand with second suit (over major: other minor)

unusual2nt-resp.name: Unusual 2NT Responses
unusual2nt-resp.description: After our 2NT unusual (5-5 minors over major; other minor+either major over minor). Weak or strong.

meaning.description: Pass or correct to lower suit — to play
Pass or correct to higher suit — to play
Invitational+ with higher known suit — strong hand interest

1nt-doubled.name: 1NT Doubled Escape
1nt-doubled.description: After our 1NT is doubled. Pass = waiting/wriggle with partner; XX = start wriggle with partner re-doubling. 2x escapes show a 5+ card suit.

meaning.description: Waiting — shows a runout hand; partner redoubles to start wriggle
Partner: shows good hand (can stand 1NTX) or starts wriggle
Wriggle: one-suiter or two-suiter with !c; partner bids 2!c which you pass or correct
